resources.string: Select up to two paired Bluetooth speakers.
Choose which speaker is left and right.
BluePair helps route audio to two configured Bluetooth speakers with quick access from the app, tile, and widget.
Configure two Bluetooth devices and channel assignment first.
Left and right speakers detected. Stereo route is active.
One configured speaker detected. Connect both for full stereo.
Waiting for both configured speakers to become active outputs.
Bluetooth permission is required to read paired devices.
Grant Bluetooth permission from Configure before enabling stereo.
Select two paired speakers on Configure before enabling stereo.
Assign distinct Left and Right speakers on Channels before enabling stereo.
Starting BluePair routing and checking connected outputs.
Waiting for both configured speakers to appear as active outputs.
Both configured speakers are active outputs. Stereo route is active.
Only one configured speaker is active. Classic Bluetooth dual output is OEM-dependent.

Both configured speakers are connected, but this phone exposes only one active classic A2DP media route at a time for this device pair. Try disabling the Phone calls profile for both speakers and, in Developer options, disabling Bluetooth A2DP hardware offload.
Only one configured speaker is active. LE Audio is detected; keep LE path as fallback.
This device exposes only one classic Bluetooth media route to apps. Dual output may require OEM Dual Audio settings.
LE Audio profile is visible but dual media outputs are not exposed yet. Check Bluetooth system settings.
Configure two paired speakers and assign Left/Right before enabling.
Bluetooth permission is required before enabling routing.
Open Bluetooth settings and enable your OEM dual-audio control.
Inspect captured routing snapshots and copy them for debugging.
No diagnostics captured yet. Try enabling routing first.
OEM automation requested. Opened Bluetooth settings and waiting for accessibility flow.
OEM automation tapped a matching dual-audio control. Re-run routing probe now.
Automates Bluetooth settings navigation for OEM dual-audio controls.
No OEM diff captured yet. Mark baseline, change OEM setting, then capture diff.
No baseline found. Mark baseline first.
Shizuku binder not running. Start Shizuku app first.
Shizuku permission requested. Accept the prompt in Shizuku.
Shizuku app not installed. Install Shizuku Manager first.
Opened Shizuku app. Start Shizuku (wireless debugging or adb mode), then return and request permission again.
